Rockefeller Capital Management
Corporate Visibility
Platform Benchmark
National Assessment — 5 Markets — May 15, 2026
National Platform Assessment
MILEAGE
01
Executive
Summary
Where rockco.com stands across 5 U.S. markets — and what the data reveals about the structural gap.
13K
rockco.com Organic Keywords
62K
Competitor Avg. Organic KWs
5
Markets Assessed
466K
Aggregate Monthly Search Vol.

Rockefeller Capital Management's SEO visibility gap stems from a fundamental structural issue: the complete absence of location-based page architecture. While competitors leverage dedicated location hierarchies to capture local search traffic, Rockefeller operates without /locations/, state pages, or city pages.

The impact is measurable across all benchmarked markets. Rockefeller ranks on Page 1 for zero keywords out of 200 tracked terms representing 465,990 monthly searches. Competitors consistently secure 2-8 Page 1 rankings per market, with top performers like Creative Planning generating 56,375 monthly visits from 36,848 organic keywords.

A structured location architecture could unlock significant organic visibility for Rockefeller's 46 US offices. The data demonstrates clear demand exists—competitors are successfully capturing this traffic through dedicated location pages that Rockefeller currently cannot compete for without the foundational site structure.

Data source: SEMRush • 200 keywords tracked across 5 markets • Generated May 15, 2026
Rockefeller Capital Management Corporate Visibility Platform Benchmark
MILEAGE
02
Domain Authority
Comparison

Domain-level metrics from SEMRush show how each competitor’s organic footprint compares to rockco.com at the national level. These numbers reflect total indexed visibility — not market-specific rankings.

Metric rockco.com Mariner Wealth Creative Planning Hightower Merrill Lynch Cresset Capital
Organic Keywords 13K 48K 37K 7.4K 206K 10K
Organic Traffic (mo.) 23K 30K 56K 9.6K 1.4M 13K
Organic Traffic Cost $44K $100K $287K $30K $6.9M $256K
SEMRush Rank 84K 67K 38K 188K 1.9K 142K
What This Tells Us

Domain-level metrics reveal Rockefeller's limited organic footprint compared to competitors who have invested in SEO infrastructure. While rockco.com ranks for 12,682 keywords generating 23,484 monthly visits, Creative Planning captures 56,375 visits from 36,848 keywords and Mariner Wealth generates 29,959 visits from 47,849 keywords. These competitors have built comprehensive content strategies that Rockefeller has not yet implemented.

Rockefeller Capital Management Corporate Visibility Platform Benchmark
MILEAGE
03
Architecture
Comparison

Competitors who rank in local search share a common pattern: they build dedicated location pages. Rockefeller’s site has no location hierarchy — it relies on flat team pages with no geographic structure for search engines to index.

Rockefeller
rockco.com
Flat — No Location Hierarchy
rockco.com/
  ├── /team-name/
  ├── /team-name/
  └── /team-name/
No state, city, or service+city pages
Mariner Wealth
marinerwealthadvisors.com
Hierarchical — State → City
/locations/
  ├── /locations/michigan/
  │   └── /locations/michigan/grand-rapids/
  ├── /locations/texas/
  │   └── /locations/texas/dallas/
Creative Planning
creativeplanning.com
Metro-Area Pages
/locations/
  ├── /locations/grand-rapids-mi/
  ├── /locations/chicago-il/
  ├── /locations/charlotte-nc/
  └── /locations/dallas-tx/
Hightower Advisors
hightoweradvisors.com
Subdomain per Team
hightoweradvisors.com/
  ├── /our-team/team-name/
  ├── /financial-advisor/city-state/
  └── /wealth-management/city-state/
The Pattern
Every competitor that ranks locally has invested in some form of location-based URL architecture. The specific approach varies — hierarchical, metro-area, or subdomain — but the principle is consistent: dedicated, indexable pages for each market. Rockefeller has none.
Rockefeller Capital Management Corporate Visibility Platform Benchmark
MILEAGE
04
Architecture
Deep-Dive
Competitor URL Pattern Example URL Est. Location Pages State Rollups City Pages Service+City
Rockefeller Flat team pages, no location hierarchy rockco.com/team-name/ 0 No No No
Mariner Wealth Hierarchical: /locations/state/city/ marinerwealthadvisors.com/locations/michigan/grand-rapids/ 150+ Yes Yes Partial
Creative Planning Metro-area pages under /locations/ creativeplanning.com/locations/grand-rapids-mi/ 100+ No Yes No
Hightower Service+city pages, team subpages hightoweradvisors.com/financial-advisor/dallas-tx/ 80+ No Yes Yes
Merrill Lynch National portal with advisor locator ml.com/financial-goals/ 500+ Yes Yes Yes
Cresset Capital Office pages under /about/offices/ cressetcapital.com/about/offices/chicago/ 15+ No Yes No
What Location Architecture Enables

A proper location hierarchy does three things that flat team pages cannot:

1. Indexability — Google can discover and rank dedicated /city/ pages for “financial advisor [city]” queries. Team bios don’t target those terms.

2. Internal link equity — State rollup pages pass authority to city pages, which pass to individual advisor profiles. This compounds over time.

3. Service+City combinations — The highest-converting keywords pair a service with a city (“wealth management Dallas”). Without dedicated pages, there’s nothing to rank.

Rockefeller Capital Management Corporate Visibility Platform Benchmark
MILEAGE
11
Opportunity
Sizing

Translating the visibility gap into business terms. These estimates are based on competitor organic traffic data from SEMRush and industry-standard click-through rates for top-10 Google positions.

302K
Avg. Competitor Monthly Organic Visits
Estimated organic traffic competitors capture that rockco.com does not
$1.5M
CPC-Equivalent Monthly Value
What competitors would pay for equivalent traffic through Google Ads
50–200
Avg. Organic KWs per Competitor Location Page
What a dedicated location page typically earns in indexed keywords

Competitors are capturing substantial traffic that Rockefeller cannot access—Creative Planning's 56,375 monthly visits and Mariner Wealth's 29,959 visits demonstrate the revenue potential of local SEO investment. Even smaller competitors like Cresset Capital generate 13,101 monthly visits from location-focused strategies.

A Corp → State → Office → City architecture spanning 46 offices would create hundreds of location-specific landing pages. This foundation would enable Rockefeller to compete for the 465,990+ monthly searches currently dominated by competitors, transforming zero local rankings into measurable organic lead generation.

Rockefeller Capital Management Corporate Visibility Platform Benchmark
MILEAGE
12
Recommended
Architecture

The proposed location hierarchy gives every Rockefeller office a dedicated, indexable presence. Each level serves a specific SEO and user-experience function.

Corporate Hub
/locations/
→
National directory of all 46 offices. Internal link equity hub. Targets “Rockefeller offices” queries.
State Pages
/locations/texas/
→
Rollup page per state. Aggregates offices, passes authority down. Targets “financial advisor [state]” terms.
Office Pages
/locations/texas/dallas/
→
Dedicated page per office with team, address, services. Primary ranking target for “financial advisor [city]”.
Service + City
/locations/texas/dallas/wealth-management/
→
Service-specific pages per city. Targets high-converting “[service] [city]” keywords.
What Mileage Delivers
Web Design
Development
SEO Architecture
Content Strategy
Directory Management
Mileage designs, builds, and manages the full location platform: site architecture, page templates, local content, directory listings, and ongoing SEO. The result is a compounding organic asset that drives visibility across every market Rockefeller serves.
